nation: Conseil de la Nation Innu Matimekush – Lac John
one_time_description: Gain sur radiation de dettes (debt forgiveness gain)
economic_notes: GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Transport Ferroviaire Tshiuetin inc. (33.33%), Tshiuetin S.E.C., Distribution Pétrolière Naskinnu S.E.C., Pétrole Naskinnuk S.E.C. (31%), Hôtel Innu S.E.C. (100%), Béton Naskinnu S.E.C. (100%), Dépanneur Innu inc. (100%). Significant mining royalties of $15,025,419 received in 2021 vs $2,141,009 in 2020.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to audit completeness of gasoline sales revenues. Significant prior period adjustments of $2,086,937 in 2021 and ($2,661,646) in 2020 related to late progress reports rejected by funders. Large one-time gain on debt forgiveness of $4,701,847. Mining royalties jumped dramatically from $2.1M to $15M. Seven GBEs accounted for by modified equity method with net negative equity contribution of ($658,320). Financial statements are in French and prepared under Canadian Public Sector Accounting Standards. ISC funding calculated as SAC fixed+flexible contributions ($16,775,698 + $2,466,553). Total government transfers include SAC, Institut Tshakapesh, Santé Canada, SAA, Fonds d'habitation, SCHL. Segment expenses from Annexe 3 sectorial breakdown. Deferred revenue of $14,721,321 is classified as current liability on balance sheet.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
